concept: Format / Chosen Form in Which Content Is Presented
description: The chosen sensory, linguistic, spatial, or technical form in which content is presented and received. The same message may be spoken, written, captioned, or made tactile, yet its wording and practical effect can still differ. Exact file types, encodings, and named communication systems remain source terms when their identity matters.
sound_symbolism: Fine bilabial breath opens 'phe' into balanced 'e'. The tongue flows through stressed 'lo', then final 'tu' sets a clean dental stop before deep 'u'. A soft opening rounds at its center and ends on a small firm contact.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'mia ha phelotu kanu.' (I choose this format.) A possessor may name the content, while adjectives can identify tactile, legible, audible, or accessible properties. Distinct from 'haluma' (language), 'phelui' (word), and 'norae' (method).

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Form changes how information is encountered; a page under the fingers and a voice in a room ask different things of the body.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Naming the format makes it possible to ask whether communication reached someone in a form they could use.

semantic_domains.dialogue: a communication form selected for sending and receiving meaning
semantic_domains.physical: a sensory form such as audible, visual, or tactile presentation
